queries.query: ok our cli's --help output is dense af, our team keeps complaining new joiners can't figure out what --region vs --zone vs --cluster actually does. can you take a pass at the help layout and tell me what to tighten?
users keep reporting they retry our webhooks because our endpoint sometimes returns HTTP 200 with a body like {error: 'invalid signature'}. how do i explain why that's wrong and what to look at?

before we cut 2.0 of our grpc billing service, sanity-check that we have a deprecation window for the v1 paths and that our migration docs cover what an integrator has to do. ~30 enterprise customers still on v1
i have this onboarding doc for new contributors to our open-source repo (8 steps from clone to first PR). where will new people get stuck? we keep losing first-time contributors
customer keeps hitting `auth failed` after they rotate api keys in our dashboard. they swear they're using the new key. what should we look at?
our developers complain pnpm install takes 45s, the rollup build is 2m, jest is 8m. before we throw budget at it, what's worth investing in first from a dx perspective?
verify whether DX-CLI-002 is fixed using docs/audits/dx-audit-workflow-state-2026-05-20-cli.json and this PR diff
our README is mostly badges and marketing copy and people keep asking us in support what the install command is. can you audit it for first-impressions stuff?
do a 30-second basics pass on this repo — working README install snippet, a LICENSE, a CHANGELOG, --help and --version actually working. quick gut check before we ship
customers blow up because `npm install our-sdk` pulls in 80MB of transitive deps and our peer deps aren't declared so it silently breaks on react 17. audit the package surface
users say our cli's --verbose flag dumps json on top of regular output and they can't grep it, plus they think it's leaking their api key. look at our logging UX
our CHANGELOG.md is basically auto-generated commit messages and breaking changes are buried with no callout. how do we fix the changelog surface?
users on alpine linux keep saying our pip install fails because libxml2 isn't a declared system requirement and the error just says 'gcc not found'. is this package or setup?
run a pre-1.0 risk pass on our CLI before we release — fresh machine, env skew, creds, version skew, destructive ops
we're shipping a 1.0 of a public SDK next quarter — what api contracts and surface decisions should i pin down before 1.0 so we don't end up with a hyrum's-law nightmare?
i want to redesign the error envelope for our REST API from scratch — what shape should it take if i want clients to handle retries and idempotency cleanly?
design how our cli should read config — file vs env vs flag precedence, where the config file lives per-OS, how we validate. what should we get right up front?
our consumer signup form at example.com/signup has a 60% drop-off. help me reduce friction on the 4-step form
audit the accessibility of our checkout page for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance, here's the page html
the production database is hot at 2pm every day on our reporting query, help me trace what's causing it. postgres 14 with pgbouncer
explain how OAuth2 PKCE flow works — i'm writing a one-pager for our security team
refactor this typescript function to use early returns instead of nested if statements
